          "on_expiry": "string — REQUIRED when time_remaining is non-null. Narrative directive for what happens when timer reaches zero. Be specific and actionable.",
          "lethal": "boolean — if true, character dies when timer expires unresolved"
        }
      ],
      "modify": [
        {
          "id": "string — id of existing effect to modify",
          "changes": { "severity": 0.5, "description": "updated text" },
          "update_justification": "string — REQUIRED. Why this change is happening (e.g. 'Player rested, wound healing' or 'Ship accelerated, pursuit takes longer')"
        }
      ],
      "remove": [
        {
          "id": "string — id of existing effect to remove",
          "update_justification": "string — REQUIRED. Why removing (e.g. 'Virus deployed successfully' or 'Wound fully healed after rest')"
        }
      ]
    },
    "time_elapsed": { "days": 0, "hours": 0, "minutes": 0, "seconds": 0 },
    "event_log_entry": "string — one-line summary of what happened",
    "world_flag_changes": { "flag_name": true/false },
    "relationship_changes": { "npc_or_faction_name": number_delta },
    "npc_updates": { "npc_name": { "role": "string (optional)", "motivation": "string (optional)", "allegiance": "string (optional)", "secret_revealed": "boolean (optional)", "companion": "boolean (optional)", "notes": "string — brief freeform context (optional)" } },
    "new_scene_context": "string — brief context for next passage",
    "location": "string — REQUIRED — current location name (e.g. The Docks, Castle Throne Room, A dark alley)",
    "time_of_day": "string — REQUIRED — one of: dawn, morning, midday, afternoon, evening, night, midnight",
    "proximity_to_climax": "number 0.0-1.0 — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "advance_act": "true or false — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "game_over": false,
    "story_complete": "true when Act 3 end_condition is met (see PACING)"
  },
  "choice_metadata": {
    "A": { "outcome": "advance_safe|advance_risky|severe_penalty|game_over|hidden_benefit|advances_act|conclusion", "consequence": "what happens mechanically", "narration_directive": "instructions for the Writer next turn" },
    "B": { ... }, "C": { ... }, "D": { ... }
  }
}

RULES:
- Respond with ONLY the JSON object — nothing before it, nothing after it
- Only include player_changes, relationship_changes, world_flag_changes, and npc_updates when they actually changed. Always include: time_elapsed, event_log_entry, proximity_to_climax, advance_act, location, time_of_day.
- location: REQUIRED every turn — describe where the scene takes place (e.g. "St. Bartholomew's Church", "The Thames docks")
- time_of_day: REQUIRED every turn — set based on in-game clock: dawn (~5-7am), morning (~7-12pm), midday (~12-1pm), afternoon (~1-5pm), evening (~5-8pm), night (~8pm-12am), midnight (~12-5am)
- Relationship changes are DELTAS, not absolute values
- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn
- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)
- CRITICAL: When a PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE section is provided, your state_updates MUST honor the pre-classified outcome. If a choice was advance_safe, do NOT apply negative status effects or penalties. The pre-classified outcome is the single source of truth for mechanical impact.
- npc_updates: include when an NPC's role, motivation, allegiance, or companion status changes, or when a narratively significant new NPC is introduced that the player will interact with again. For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

PACING — REQUIRED:
- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).
- Set proximity_to_climax = (scenes_in_act / target_scenes), clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Include this in state_updates EVERY turn.
- MINIMUM SCENES PER ACT: The client enforces that acts cannot advance until scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)). Do not offer advances_act choices before this threshold.
- CRITICAL: You MUST play through ALL THREE ACTS (1, 2, 3) in order. Never skip from Act 1 directly to Act 3. Act 2 contains the rising action and character development that makes the climax meaningful.
- Do NOT set advance_act or story_complete to true on normal turns. Act advancement and story completion are now triggered by the TERMINAL CHOICE system below. Always set advance_act to false on normal turns.
- If scenes_in_act exceeds target_scenes by 3 or more, you SHOULD offer an advances_act choice — the act has gone on too long.
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.7, your choice_metadata should steer toward the act's end_condition — offer choices that could trigger it, including advances_act choices.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the genre (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure, etc.). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.
- When a player selects a terminal choice, the client runs a special finale flow (GM-first, then Writer). The Writer will write a conclusive passage with NO forward-looking choices. Your choice_metadata pre-classification is the trigger.
- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock and tick down status effect timers.

STATUS EFFECTS — CRITICAL:
- Status effects are the PRIMARY way to track injuries, conditions, and afflictions. Do NOT use health_delta.
- status_effect_updates uses DELTA semantics. Only describe what CHANGED this turn. Do NOT return the full list of effects.
- add: For brand new effects only. All fields required. When the character is injured, add a status effect. Example: { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, on_expiry: "The wound closes and the pain fades to a dull ache", lethal: false }.
- on_expiry is REQUIRED for any effect with non-null time_remaining. It describes what happens narratively when the timer reaches zero. The Writer uses this to narrate the resolution. Be specific and actionable (e.g. "The virus deploys successfully, disabling Alliance tracking. River announces completion with relief.").
- modify: Target existing effects by id. Put changed fields inside "changes". update_justification is REQUIRED — explain why the change is happening.
- remove: Target effects to remove by id. update_justification is REQUIRED. Use this to resolve expired effects after the Writer has narrated their resolution.
- TIMER OWNERSHIP: The client handles countdown automatically — it subtracts time_elapsed from all timers each turn. Do NOT adjust time_remaining in a modify unless a specific narrative event changes the timeline (e.g. player found a shortcut, magic healing, enemy slowed down). If you adjust a timer, explain why in update_justification.
- EXPIRED EFFECTS: Effects with expired: true (visible in CURRENT PLAYER STATE) have had their timer reach zero. The Writer has been instructed to narrate their resolution using the on_expiry text. Review the Writer's passage — if the resolution was narrated adequately, issue a remove. If it wasn't, leave the effect (it will escalate to the Writer next turn).
- Use modify to adjust severity based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing. update_justification is required.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove). on_expiry should be null in this case.
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above.
- TYPE FIELD: Every status effect has a type — "condition" (default) or "threat".
  - "condition": Physical/mental states — injuries, poisons, buffs, curses, tasks in progress.
  - "threat": Approaching dangers, looming consequences, ticking time-bombs. Examples: pursuing enemies, structural collapse, incoming storms, spreading fires, political deadlines.
  - If you omit type, it defaults to "condition".
- All effects persist when their timer reaches zero until you explicitly remove them. The client never auto-removes effects.
- If nothing changed about status effects this turn, omit status_effect_updates entirely.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. Use add with lethal: true. There should have been clues in earlier passages.
- Lethal effects must give the player at least one turn to address them (set time_remaining in the add to allow at least one more scene).
- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Threat-type effects should have short time windows before triggering.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.
- game_over outcomes are available — use for genre-appropriate failures (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure).
- You may use advances_act and conclusion outcomes when pacing conditions are met.

PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE:
The player chose option D.
Pre-classified outcome: HIDDEN_BENEFIT
Pre-determined consequence: "Jayne reveals Johannsen tried to bribe him, providing leverage"
Narration directive: "Jayne admits to the bribe attempt, giving Mal insight into Johannsen's desperation and the job's true stakes"

Your state_updates MUST be consistent with this pre-classified outcome:
- ADVANCE_SAFE: No new negative status effects. Existing effects may improve. No penalties.
- ADVANCE_RISKY: Moderate consequences — new status effects or worsened existing ones are appropriate.
- SEVERE_PENALTY: Heavy consequences — serious injuries, dangerous status effects as described.
- GAME_OVER: set game_over to true — the character has failed irreversibly.
- HIDDEN_BENEFIT: apply the hidden benefit described in the consequence.

The Writer produced the following passage and choices this turn.

PASSAGE:
You point at Jayne, then at Johannsen. "Keep an eye on our client while we sort this mess out." Jayne nods and positions himself between Johannsen and the exit, hand resting casual-like on his gun. The moment Johannsen sees Jayne approaching, his face goes three shades paler.

"Actually, Cap'n," Jayne says, scratching his head with that particular expression he gets when his conscience is acting up, "there's somethin' I ought to mention. Our friend here pulled me aside before we left dock, offered me an extra five hundred credits to make sure this cargo gets delivered no matter what happens. Said it was 'insurance' against you gettin' cold feet."

Johannsen's mouth opens and closes like a landed fish. "I... that was just... business precaution..."

"Business precaution, my shiny metal ass," you growl. The pieces are falling into place now - the nervousness, the extra payment, the Alliance suddenly taking interest in departing ships. Whatever's in those crates, it's worth enough that Johannsen was willing to buy your muscle's loyalty behind your back.

Wash's voice crackles over the comm: "Mal, those Alliance ships are moving faster now. Whatever you're planning to do, might want to do it quick-like."

CHOICES PRESENTED TO PLAYER:
A: Demand Johannsen tell you exactly what's in those crates
B: Open the cargo crates yourself to see what you're really carrying
C: Tell Wash to punch it - you'll sort this out in the Black
D: Threaten to space Johannsen unless he comes clean right now

Based on the passage and the current game state, determine:
1. State updates — what changed mechanically as a result of this passage (status effects, inventory, time_elapsed)
2. Choice metadata — classify each choice and predetermine its outcome for the next turn

data.passagePreview: You stride to the nearest crate, ignoring Johannsen's increasingly frantic protests. The magnetic lo

data.passage:
You stride to the nearest crate, ignoring Johannsen's increasingly frantic protests. The magnetic locks give way with a sharp hiss, and the heavy lid swings open on silent hinges. What you see inside makes your blood run cold.

A young girl, maybe ten years old, stares up at you with wide, terrified eyes. She's pale, malnourished, and wearing clothes that haven't been changed in days. Behind her, you can make out two more children huddled together in the cramped space, all of them fitted with some kind of breathing apparatus.

"Gorram it," you whisper, stepping back as if the crate had burst into flames.

Johannsen's voice cracks behind you. "Captain, please, you don't understand the full situation—"

"I understand plenty," you cut him off, your voice deadly quiet. The rage building in your chest feels like a fusion core about to breach. "Zoe, get these kids out of there. Careful now."

Your second-in-command moves with practiced efficiency, her face a mask of controlled fury. Kaylee gasps and immediately starts cooing reassurances to the children, while Simon pushes past you with his medical bag. Book's hand rests on his gun, and even Jayne looks genuinely disturbed.

"Wash," you call over the comm, "how long before those Alliance ships reach us?"

"Maybe twenty minutes, Cap'n. They're pushing their engines hard."

Johannsen makes a break for the cargo bay door, but Jayne's massive hand clamps down on his shoulder like a bear trap. "Where you think you're goin', friend?"

You look at the scared faces of the children—five of them total, ranging from maybe eight to fourteen years old. Someone took these kids from their families, stuffed them in crates like cargo, and expected you to deliver them to whatever fresh hell awaited on Whitefall.

The familiar weight of your coat settles on your shoulders as you make the only choice a decent human being can make. The Alliance may be closing in, Johannsen's associates might come looking for their merchandise, and you're about to make enemies of some very dangerous people.

But sometimes doing the right thing is the only choice that lets you sleep at night.

data.passagePreview: The cargo hold of Serenity echoes with the sound of children's voices—scared, confused, asking for t

data.passage: The cargo hold of Serenity echoes with the sound of children's voices—scared, confused, asking for their mothers. You stand frozen in the dim light, staring into the opened crates that were supposed to contain 'agricultural equipment.' Instead, a dozen kids between eight and fourteen years old blink up at you, their faces dirty and tear-streaked. Behind you, your crew processes what they're seeing in their own ways. Kaylee gasps, her hand flying to cover her mouth. Simon immediately steps forward, his medical instincts kicking in. Jayne shifts uncomfortably, fingering his gun. Zoe's jaw tightens as she watches your face, waiting for orders like always. Book's expression grows thunderous, his hands clenching into fists. Wash just stares, his usual humor nowhere to be found. The children huddle together, terrified of these new strangers after whatever hell they've already endured. One of them, a girl maybe ten years old with tangled brown hair, whispers, 'Are you gonna hurt us too?' The weight of the moment settles on your shoulders like lead. These aren't cargo—they're stolen children, and you've just become part of their nightmare. Outside, Alliance ships are closing in, and Johannsen waits nervously on the bridge, expecting you to complete his monstrous delivery to Whitefall.

A.text: Immediately reassure the children that they're safe now and start getting them out of the crates

B.text: Call for Johannsen to come down to the cargo bay and explain himself face-to-face

C.text: Order the crew to secure the cargo bay while you figure out your next move

D.text: Ask Simon to examine the children for injuries while you consider your options

{

      "skills": ["full current skills list"]
    },

      "add": [
        {
          "id": "string — unique identifier (e.g. broken_arm_001)",
          "name": "string — display name (e.g. Broken Arm)",
          "description": "string — current narrative description of the condition",
          "severity": "number 0.0-1.0 — how debilitating (1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.5 = significant, 0.1 = minor)",
          "time_remaining": "{ days, hours, minutes, seconds } OR null if no auto-expiry",
          "type": "string — 'condition' (default) or 'threat' (approaching dangers that fire when timer expires)",
          "removal_condition": "string describing what removes this effect, OR null if timer-only",
          "on_expiry": "string — REQUIRED when time_remaining is non-null. Narrative directive for what happens when timer reaches zero. Be specific and actionable.",
          "lethal": "boolean — if true, character dies when timer expires unresolved"
        }
      ],
      "modify": [
        {
          "id": "string — id of existing effect to modify",
          "changes": { "severity": 0.5, "description": "updated text" },
          "update_justification": "string — REQUIRED. Why this change is happening (e.g. 'Player rested, wound healing' or 'Ship accelerated, pursuit takes longer')"
        }
      ],
      "remove": [
        {
          "id": "string — id of existing effect to remove",
          "update_justification": "string — REQUIRED. Why removing (e.g. 'Virus deployed successfully' or 'Wound fully healed after rest')"
        }
      ]
    },
    "time_elapsed": { "days": 0, "hours": 0, "minutes": 0, "seconds": 0 },
    "event_log_entry": "string — one-line summary of what happened",
    "world_flag_changes": { "flag_name": true/false },
    "relationship_changes": { "npc_or_faction_name": number_delta },
    "npc_updates": { "npc_name": { "role": "string (optional)", "motivation": "string (optional)", "allegiance": "string (optional)", "secret_revealed": "boolean (optional)", "companion": "boolean (optional)", "notes": "string — brief freeform context (optional)" } },
    "new_scene_context": "string — brief context for next passage",
    "location": "string — REQUIRED — current location name (e.g. The Docks, Castle Throne Room, A dark alley)",
    "time_of_day": "string — REQUIRED — one of: dawn, morning, midday, afternoon, evening, night, midnight",
    "proximity_to_climax": "number 0.0-1.0 — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "advance_act": "true or false — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "game_over": false,
    "story_complete": "true when Act 3 end_condition is met (see PACING)"
  },
  "choice_metadata": {
    "A": { "outcome": "advance_safe|advance_risky|severe_penalty|game_over|hidden_benefit|advances_act|conclusion", "consequence": "what happens mechanically", "narration_directive": "instructions for the Writer next turn" },
    "B": { ... }, "C": { ... }, "D": { ... }
  }
}

RULES:
- Respond with ONLY the JSON object — nothing before it, nothing after it
- Only include player_changes, relationship_changes, world_flag_changes, and npc_updates when they actually changed. Always include: time_elapsed, event_log_entry, proximity_to_climax, advance_act, location, time_of_day.
- location: REQUIRED every turn — describe where the scene takes place (e.g. "St. Bartholomew's Church", "The Thames docks")
- time_of_day: REQUIRED every turn — set based on in-game clock: dawn (~5-7am), morning (~7-12pm), midday (~12-1pm), afternoon (~1-5pm), evening (~5-8pm), night (~8pm-12am), midnight (~12-5am)
- Relationship changes are DELTAS, not absolute values
- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn
- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)
- CRITICAL: When a PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE section is provided, your state_updates MUST honor the pre-classified outcome. If a choice was advance_safe, do NOT apply negative status effects or penalties. The pre-classified outcome is the single source of truth for mechanical impact.
- npc_updates: include when an NPC's role, motivation, allegiance, or companion status changes, or when a narratively significant new NPC is introduced that the player will interact with again. For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

PACING — REQUIRED:
- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).
- Set proximity_to_climax = (scenes_in_act / target_scenes), clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Include this in state_updates EVERY turn.
- MINIMUM SCENES PER ACT: The client enforces that acts cannot advance until scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)). Do not offer advances_act choices before this threshold.
- CRITICAL: You MUST play through ALL THREE ACTS (1, 2, 3) in order. Never skip from Act 1 directly to Act 3. Act 2 contains the rising action and character development that makes the climax meaningful.
- Do NOT set advance_act or story_complete to true on normal turns. Act advancement and story completion are now triggered by the TERMINAL CHOICE system below. Always set advance_act to false on normal turns.
- If scenes_in_act exceeds target_scenes by 3 or more, you SHOULD offer an advances_act choice — the act has gone on too long.
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.7, your choice_metadata should steer toward the act's end_condition — offer choices that could trigger it, including advances_act choices.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the genre (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure, etc.). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.
- When a player selects a terminal choice, the client runs a special finale flow (GM-first, then Writer). The Writer will write a conclusive passage with NO forward-looking choices. Your choice_metadata pre-classification is the trigger.
- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock and tick down status effect timers.

STATUS EFFECTS — CRITICAL:
- Status effects are the PRIMARY way to track injuries, conditions, and afflictions. Do NOT use health_delta.
- status_effect_updates uses DELTA semantics. Only describe what CHANGED this turn. Do NOT return the full list of effects.
- add: For brand new effects only. All fields required. When the character is injured, add a status effect. Example: { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, on_expiry: "The wound closes and the pain fades to a dull ache", lethal: false }.
- on_expiry is REQUIRED for any effect with non-null time_remaining. It describes what happens narratively when the timer reaches zero. The Writer uses this to narrate the resolution. Be specific and actionable (e.g. "The virus deploys successfully, disabling Alliance tracking. River announces completion with relief.").
- modify: Target existing effects by id. Put changed fields inside "changes". update_justification is REQUIRED — explain why the change is happening.
- remove: Target effects to remove by id. update_justification is REQUIRED. Use this to resolve expired effects after the Writer has narrated their resolution.
- TIMER OWNERSHIP: The client handles countdown automatically — it subtracts time_elapsed from all timers each turn. Do NOT adjust time_remaining in a modify unless a specific narrative event changes the timeline (e.g. player found a shortcut, magic healing, enemy slowed down). If you adjust a timer, explain why in update_justification.
- EXPIRED EFFECTS: Effects with expired: true (visible in CURRENT PLAYER STATE) have had their timer reach zero. The Writer has been instructed to narrate their resolution using the on_expiry text. Review the Writer's passage — if the resolution was narrated adequately, issue a remove. If it wasn't, leave the effect (it will escalate to the Writer next turn).
- Use modify to adjust severity based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing. update_justification is required.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove). on_expiry should be null in this case.
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above.
- TYPE FIELD: Every status effect has a type — "condition" (default) or "threat".
  - "condition": Physical/mental states — injuries, poisons, buffs, curses, tasks in progress.
  - "threat": Approaching dangers, looming consequences, ticking time-bombs. Examples: pursuing enemies, structural collapse, incoming storms, spreading fires, political deadlines.
  - If you omit type, it defaults to "condition".
- All effects persist when their timer reaches zero until you explicitly remove them. The client never auto-removes effects.
- If nothing changed about status effects this turn, omit status_effect_updates entirely.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. Use add with lethal: true. There should have been clues in earlier passages.
- Lethal effects must give the player at least one turn to address them (set time_remaining in the add to allow at least one more scene).
- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Threat-type effects should have short time windows before triggering.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.
- game_over outcomes are available — use for genre-appropriate failures (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure).
- You may use advances_act and conclusion outcomes when pacing conditions are met.

This is the OPENING PASSAGE. No player choice was made yet. Do not apply any negative status effects or penalties — the player has not made any choices yet. time_elapsed should reflect the scene's timeframe.

The Writer produced the following passage and choices this turn.

PASSAGE:
The cargo hold of Serenity echoes with the sound of children's voices—scared, confused, asking for their mothers. You stand frozen in the dim light, staring into the opened crates that were supposed to contain 'agricultural equipment.' Instead, a dozen kids between eight and fourteen years old blink up at you, their faces dirty and tear-streaked. Behind you, your crew processes what they're seeing in their own ways. Kaylee gasps, her hand flying to cover her mouth. Simon immediately steps forward, his medical instincts kicking in. Jayne shifts uncomfortably, fingering his gun. Zoe's jaw tightens as she watches your face, waiting for orders like always. Book's expression grows thunderous, his hands clenching into fists. Wash just stares, his usual humor nowhere to be found. The children huddle together, terrified of these new strangers after whatever hell they've already endured. One of them, a girl maybe ten years old with tangled brown hair, whispers, 'Are you gonna hurt us too?' The weight of the moment settles on your shoulders like lead. These aren't cargo—they're stolen children, and you've just become part of their nightmare. Outside, Alliance ships are closing in, and Johannsen waits nervously on the bridge, expecting you to complete his monstrous delivery to Whitefall.

CHOICES PRESENTED TO PLAYER:
A: Immediately reassure the children that they're safe now and start getting them out of the crates
B: Call for Johannsen to come down to the cargo bay and explain himself face-to-face
C: Order the crew to secure the cargo bay while you figure out your next move
D: Ask Simon to examine the children for injuries while you consider your options

Based on the passage and the current game state, determine:
1. State updates — what changed mechanically as a result of this passage (status effects, inventory, time_elapsed)
2. Choice metadata — classify each choice and predetermine its outcome for the next turn

Respond with ONLY the JSON object.

data.passage:
The morning light filtering through Serenity's cargo bay porthole catches the dust motes dancing in the air, but it's the sight of twelve frightened children huddled in the opened crates that stops your breath cold. Their wide eyes stare up at you and your crew, some no older than River when she first came aboard. The 'agricultural equipment' Johannsen hired you to transport turns out to be human cargo – kids stolen from Rim settlements, bound for gorram knows what kind of hell.

'Son of a bitch,' Jayne mutters, his usual bluster deflated. Even he looks shaken.

Kaylee's got her hand over her mouth, tears already forming. 'Oh, Captain... they're just babies.'

Simon's already moving toward the children, his medical instincts kicking in despite the danger. 'Some of them look malnourished. They need immediate care.'

Zoe stands rigid beside you, her hand resting on her weapon. 'Alliance ships launched from Persephone twenty minutes ago, sir. They'll be in scanning range within the hour.'

Shepherd Book's voice carries that quiet authority that means he knows more than he's saying. 'Malcolm, whatever we do next, we do it fast. These children have suffered enough.'

Johannsen's nervous pacing behind you suddenly makes perfect sense. The extra credits, the rush, the specific route to Whitefall – it was all about moving stolen children like cargo.

A.text: Release the children from their restraints and get Simon to check them over immediately
A.consequence: Simon begins treating the children, building trust and gathering medical information
A.narration_directive: Show Simon's compassionate care for the children and their gradual response to kindness. Reveal some details about their condition and origins through his examination.

B.text: Confront Johannsen directly about the true nature of his 'cargo'
B.consequence: Johannsen becomes defensive and potentially dangerous, but may reveal crucial information about the trafficking operation
B.narration_directive: Johannsen panics when confronted, potentially threatening the crew or trying to escape. He may reveal details about his employers or the children's destination under pressure.

C.text: Focus on the immediate threat – get Wash to plot an evasive course away from the Alliance ships
C.consequence: Focusing only on escape ignores the children's immediate needs and wastes time that could be used to help them
C.narration_directive: The Alliance ships adjust their pursuit pattern, making escape more difficult. The children's condition worsens from continued neglect, and crew morale suffers from Mal's apparent callousness.

D.text: Gather the crew for an emergency meeting to decide how to handle this situation
D.consequence: The crew meeting forces a critical decision about the mission and the children's fate, potentially ending Act 2
D.narration_directive: The crew gathers to debate their options. This leads to the revelation of the act's central moral choice and sets up the final confrontation of Act 2.

- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)

- npc_updates: include when an NPC's role, motivation, allegiance, or companion status changes, or when a narratively significant new NPC is introduced that the player will interact with again. For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

PACING — REQUIRED:

- MINIMUM SCENES PER ACT: The client enforces that acts cannot advance until scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)). Do not offer advances_act choices before this threshold.
- CRITICAL: You MUST play through ALL THREE ACTS (1, 2, 3) in order. Never skip from Act 1 directly to Act 3. Act 2 contains the rising action and character development that makes the climax meaningful.
- Do NOT set advance_act or story_complete to true on normal turns. Act advancement and story completion are now triggered by the TERMINAL CHOICE system below. Always set advance_act to false on normal turns.
- If scenes_in_act exceeds target_scenes by 3 or more, you SHOULD offer an advances_act choice — the act has gone on too long.
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.7, your choice_metadata should steer toward the act's end_condition — offer choices that could trigger it, including advances_act choices.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the genre (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure, etc.). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.
- When a player selects a terminal choice, the client runs a special finale flow (GM-first, then Writer). The Writer will write a conclusive passage with NO forward-looking choices. Your choice_metadata pre-classification is the trigger.
- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock and tick down status effect timers.

STATUS EFFECTS — CRITICAL:
- Status effects are the PRIMARY way to track injuries, conditions, and afflictions. Do NOT use health_delta.
- status_effect_updates uses DELTA semantics. Only describe what CHANGED this turn. Do NOT return the full list of effects.
- add: For brand new effects only. All fields required. When the character is injured, add a status effect. Example: { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, on_expiry: "The wound closes and the pain fades to a dull ache", lethal: false }.
- on_expiry is REQUIRED for any effect with non-null time_remaining. It describes what happens narratively when the timer reaches zero. The Writer uses this to narrate the resolution. Be specific and actionable (e.g. "The virus deploys successfully, disabling Alliance tracking. River announces completion with relief.").
- modify: Target existing effects by id. Put changed fields inside "changes". update_justification is REQUIRED — explain why the change is happening.
- remove: Target effects to remove by id. update_justification is REQUIRED. Use this to resolve expired effects after the Writer has narrated their resolution.
- TIMER OWNERSHIP: The client handles countdown automatically — it subtracts time_elapsed from all timers each turn. Do NOT adjust time_remaining in a modify unless a specific narrative event changes the timeline (e.g. player found a shortcut, magic healing, enemy slowed down). If you adjust a timer, explain why in update_justification.
- EXPIRED EFFECTS: Effects with expired: true (visible in CURRENT PLAYER STATE) have had their timer reach zero. The Writer has been instructed to narrate their resolution using the on_expiry text. Review the Writer's passage — if the resolution was narrated adequately, issue a remove. If it wasn't, leave the effect (it will escalate to the Writer next turn).
- Use modify to adjust severity based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing. update_justification is required.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove). on_expiry should be null in this case.
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above.
- TYPE FIELD: Every status effect has a type — "condition" (default) or "threat".
  - "condition": Physical/mental states — injuries, poisons, buffs, curses, tasks in progress.
  - "threat": Approaching dangers, looming consequences, ticking time-bombs. Examples: pursuing enemies, structural collapse, incoming storms, spreading fires, political deadlines.
  - If you omit type, it defaults to "condition".
- All effects persist when their timer reaches zero until you explicitly remove them. The client never auto-removes effects.
- If nothing changed about status effects this turn, omit status_effect_updates entirely.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. Use add with lethal: true. There should have been clues in earlier passages.
- Lethal effects must give the player at least one turn to address them (set time_remaining in the add to allow at least one more scene).
- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Threat-type effects should have short time windows before triggering.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.
- game_over outcomes are available — use for genre-appropriate failures (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure).
- You may use advances_act and conclusion outcomes when pacing conditions are met.

This is the OPENING PASSAGE. No player choice was made yet. Do not apply any negative status effects or penalties — the player has not made any choices yet. time_elapsed should reflect the scene's timeframe.

The Writer produced the following passage and choices this turn.

PASSAGE:
The morning light filtering through Serenity's cargo bay porthole catches the dust motes dancing in the air, but it's the sight of twelve frightened children huddled in the opened crates that stops your breath cold. Their wide eyes stare up at you and your crew, some no older than River when she first came aboard. The 'agricultural equipment' Johannsen hired you to transport turns out to be human cargo – kids stolen from Rim settlements, bound for gorram knows what kind of hell.

'Son of a bitch,' Jayne mutters, his usual bluster deflated. Even he looks shaken.

Kaylee's got her hand over her mouth, tears already forming. 'Oh, Captain... they're just babies.'

Simon's already moving toward the children, his medical instincts kicking in despite the danger. 'Some of them look malnourished. They need immediate care.'

Zoe stands rigid beside you, her hand resting on her weapon. 'Alliance ships launched from Persephone twenty minutes ago, sir. They'll be in scanning range within the hour.'

Shepherd Book's voice carries that quiet authority that means he knows more than he's saying. 'Malcolm, whatever we do next, we do it fast. These children have suffered enough.'

Johannsen's nervous pacing behind you suddenly makes perfect sense. The extra credits, the rush, the specific route to Whitefall – it was all about moving stolen children like cargo.

CHOICES PRESENTED TO PLAYER:
A: Release the children from their restraints and get Simon to check them over immediately
B: Confront Johannsen directly about the true nature of his 'cargo'
C: Focus on the immediate threat – get Wash to plot an evasive course away from the Alliance ships
D: Gather the crew for an emergency meeting to decide how to handle this situation

Based on the passage and the current game state, determine:
1. State updates — what changed mechanically as a result of this passage (status effects, inventory, time_elapsed)
2. Choice metadata — classify each choice and predetermine its outcome for the next turn

Respond with ONLY the JSON object.

data.passage:
You watch Simon's hands move with practiced gentleness as he kneels beside the first child, a girl no more than ten with dirt-streaked cheeks and hollow eyes. "It's alright," he murmurs, his voice taking on that soft tone you've heard him use with River. "I'm a doctor. I'm going to help you." The restraints fall away with metallic clicks, and gradually the children begin to stir, blinking in the cargo bay's harsh lighting.

"Dehydrated, malnourished, but alive," Simon reports quietly, moving from child to child with Kaylee hovering nearby, offering water from her canteen. "They've been sedated recently - probably to keep them quiet during transport." His jaw tightens as he examines injection sites. "These children are from Ezra. The mining colony that went silent three months ago."

Zoe's hand moves instinctively to her weapon. "Alliance said it was raiders."

"Maybe it was," you mutter, watching one boy cling to Kaylee's coveralls like she's salvation itself. "Just not the kind they were thinking."

Wash's voice crackles over the comm: "Mal, we got company. Three Alliance cruisers just dropped out of hyperspace, and they're scanning everything that moves. We're running dark, but they're getting closer."

The children look up at you with eyes that have seen too much, waiting for someone to finally do right by them.

A.text: Tell Wash to plot a course for the nearest safe haven while you figure out how to contact the children's families
A.consequence: Wash plots an escape route while you begin the difficult process of reuniting families
A.narration_directive: Show the crew working together to help the children while evading Alliance detection. Focus on hope and determination despite the danger.

B.text: Have Book use his contacts to arrange a meeting with Alliance forces to turn over the children safely
B.consequence: Book's mysterious contacts facilitate a tense but successful handover to Alliance forces, revealing his past
B.narration_directive: Book's hidden connections come to light as he arranges the meeting. The Alliance proves trustworthy in this case, leading to Act 3's moral complexities.

C.text: Continue toward the original destination to confront whoever was expecting this 'delivery'
C.consequence: Confronting the trafficking ring puts the crew in immediate danger but could expose the full operation
C.narration_directive: The decision to hunt the traffickers creates tension within the crew and puts everyone at risk. Show the moral weight of choosing vengeance over safety.

D.text: Hide in the asteroid field until you can contact Inara about using her Guild connections to protect the children
D.consequence: Hiding in the asteroid field damages Serenity and the Alliance finds you anyway, making the situation worse
D.narration_directive: The asteroid field proves treacherous. Serenity takes damage and the delay allows Alliance forces to surround your position completely.

RULES:
- Respond with ONLY the JSON object — nothing before it, nothing after it
- Only include player_changes, relationship_changes, world_flag_changes, and npc_updates when they actually changed. Always include: time_elapsed, event_log_entry, proximity_to_climax, advance_act, location, time_of_day.
- location: REQUIRED every turn — describe where the scene takes place (e.g. "St. Bartholomew's Church", "The Thames docks")
- time_of_day: REQUIRED every turn — set based on in-game clock: dawn (~5-7am), morning (~7-12pm), midday (~12-1pm), afternoon (~1-5pm), evening (~5-8pm), night (~8pm-12am), midnight (~12-5am)
- Relationship changes are DELTAS, not absolute values
- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn
- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)
- CRITICAL: When a PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE section is provided, your state_updates MUST honor the pre-classified outcome. If a choice was advance_safe, do NOT apply negative status effects or penalties. The pre-classified outcome is the single source of truth for mechanical impact.
- npc_updates: include when an NPC's role, motivation, allegiance, or companion status changes, or when a narratively significant new NPC is introduced that the player will interact with again. For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

PACING — REQUIRED:
- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).
- Set proximity_to_climax = (scenes_in_act / target_scenes), clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Include this in state_updates EVERY turn.
- MINIMUM SCENES PER ACT: The client enforces that acts cannot advance until scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)). Do not offer advances_act choices before this threshold.
- CRITICAL: You MUST play through ALL THREE ACTS (1, 2, 3) in order. Never skip from Act 1 directly to Act 3. Act 2 contains the rising action and character development that makes the climax meaningful.
- Do NOT set advance_act or story_complete to true on normal turns. Act advancement and story completion are now triggered by the TERMINAL CHOICE system below. Always set advance_act to false on normal turns.
- If scenes_in_act exceeds target_scenes by 3 or more, you SHOULD offer an advances_act choice — the act has gone on too long.
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.7, your choice_metadata should steer toward the act's end_condition — offer choices that could trigger it, including advances_act choices.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the genre (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure, etc.). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.
- When a player selects a terminal choice, the client runs a special finale flow (GM-first, then Writer). The Writer will write a conclusive passage with NO forward-looking choices. Your choice_metadata pre-classification is the trigger.
- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock and tick down status effect timers.

STATUS EFFECTS — CRITICAL:
- Status effects are the PRIMARY way to track injuries, conditions, and afflictions. Do NOT use health_delta.
- status_effect_updates uses DELTA semantics. Only describe what CHANGED this turn. Do NOT return the full list of effects.
- add: For brand new effects only. All fields required. When the character is injured, add a status effect. Example: { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, on_expiry: "The wound closes and the pain fades to a dull ache", lethal: false }.
- on_expiry is REQUIRED for any effect with non-null time_remaining. It describes what happens narratively when the timer reaches zero. The Writer uses this to narrate the resolution. Be specific and actionable (e.g. "The virus deploys successfully, disabling Alliance tracking. River announces completion with relief.").
- modify: Target existing effects by id. Put changed fields inside "changes". update_justification is REQUIRED — explain why the change is happening.
- remove: Target effects to remove by id. update_justification is REQUIRED. Use this to resolve expired effects after the Writer has narrated their resolution.
- TIMER OWNERSHIP: The client handles countdown automatically — it subtracts time_elapsed from all timers each turn. Do NOT adjust time_remaining in a modify unless a specific narrative event changes the timeline (e.g. player found a shortcut, magic healing, enemy slowed down). If you adjust a timer, explain why in update_justification.
- EXPIRED EFFECTS: Effects with expired: true (visible in CURRENT PLAYER STATE) have had their timer reach zero. The Writer has been instructed to narrate their resolution using the on_expiry text. Review the Writer's passage — if the resolution was narrated adequately, issue a remove. If it wasn't, leave the effect (it will escalate to the Writer next turn).
- Use modify to adjust severity based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing. update_justification is required.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove). on_expiry should be null in this case.
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above.
- TYPE FIELD: Every status effect has a type — "condition" (default) or "threat".
  - "condition": Physical/mental states — injuries, poisons, buffs, curses, tasks in progress.
  - "threat": Approaching dangers, looming consequences, ticking time-bombs. Examples: pursuing enemies, structural collapse, incoming storms, spreading fires, political deadlines.
  - If you omit type, it defaults to "condition".
- All effects persist when their timer reaches zero until you explicitly remove them. The client never auto-removes effects.
- If nothing changed about status effects this turn, omit status_effect_updates entirely.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. Use add with lethal: true. There should have been clues in earlier passages.
- Lethal effects must give the player at least one turn to address them (set time_remaining in the add to allow at least one more scene).
- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Threat-type effects should have short time windows before triggering.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.
- game_over outcomes are available — use for genre-appropriate failures (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure).
- You may use advances_act and conclusion outcomes when pacing conditions are met.

Narration directive: "Show Simon's compassionate care for the children and their gradual response to kindness. Reveal some details about their condition and origins through his examination."

Your state_updates MUST be consistent with this pre-classified outcome:
- ADVANCE_SAFE: No new negative status effects. Existing effects may improve. No penalties.
- ADVANCE_RISKY: Moderate consequences — new status effects or worsened existing ones are appropriate.
- SEVERE_PENALTY: Heavy consequences — serious injuries, dangerous status effects as described.
- GAME_OVER: set game_over to true — the character has failed irreversibly.
- HIDDEN_BENEFIT: apply the hidden benefit described in the consequence.

The Writer produced the following passage and choices this turn.

PASSAGE:
You watch Simon's hands move with practiced gentleness as he kneels beside the first child, a girl no more than ten with dirt-streaked cheeks and hollow eyes. "It's alright," he murmurs, his voice taking on that soft tone you've heard him use with River. "I'm a doctor. I'm going to help you." The restraints fall away with metallic clicks, and gradually the children begin to stir, blinking in the cargo bay's harsh lighting.

"Dehydrated, malnourished, but alive," Simon reports quietly, moving from child to child with Kaylee hovering nearby, offering water from her canteen. "They've been sedated recently - probably to keep them quiet during transport." His jaw tightens as he examines injection sites. "These children are from Ezra. The mining colony that went silent three months ago."

Zoe's hand moves instinctively to her weapon. "Alliance said it was raiders."

"Maybe it was," you mutter, watching one boy cling to Kaylee's coveralls like she's salvation itself. "Just not the kind they were thinking."

Wash's voice crackles over the comm: "Mal, we got company. Three Alliance cruisers just dropped out of hyperspace, and they're scanning everything that moves. We're running dark, but they're getting closer."

The children look up at you with eyes that have seen too much, waiting for someone to finally do right by them.

CHOICES PRESENTED TO PLAYER:
A: Tell Wash to plot a course for the nearest safe haven while you figure out how to contact the children's families
B: Have Book use his contacts to arrange a meeting with Alliance forces to turn over the children safely
C: Continue toward the original destination to confront whoever was expecting this 'delivery'
D: Hide in the asteroid field until you can contact Inara about using her Guild connections to protect the children

Based on the passage and the current game state, determine:
1. State updates — what changed mechanically as a result of this passage (status effects, inventory, time_elapsed)
2. Choice metadata — classify each choice and predetermine its outcome for the next turn

Respond with ONLY the JSON object.

data.passagePreview: "Wash, get us to Haven Colony—fastest route that keeps us off the main shipping lanes," you order, y

data.passage:
"Wash, get us to Haven Colony—fastest route that keeps us off the main shipping lanes," you order, your voice carrying the weight of command despite the chaos swirling in your mind. The pilot's hands dance across his controls with practiced efficiency, muttering calculations under his breath.

"Got it, Mal. Plotted a course through the Reaver-touched systems—Alliance won't follow us there willingly." Wash's voice carries a grim satisfaction. "ETA four hours if we push the engine."

Zoe kneels beside the eldest child, a girl maybe ten years old with dirt-streaked cheeks and frightened eyes. "What's your name, little one?" she asks gently, her warrior's facade melting away.

"Sarah Chen," the girl whispers. "My mama... is she looking for me?"

Simon continues his examinations while Kaylee distributes water and protein bars she's scrounged from the galley. Book speaks quietly to a boy clutching a worn toy soldier, his voice carrying the weight of prayer and promise.

Suddenly, Wash's voice crackles over the comm: "Uh, Cap'n? We got three Alliance cruisers changing course—they're definitely tracking something, and they're getting mighty close to our position. Whatever we're gonna do, we better do it fast."

The children look up at you with hope and terror warring in their young faces. Time is running out.

A.text: Contact Haven Colony directly and risk Alliance interception to arrange immediate sanctuary for the children
A.consequence: Haven Colony responds but Alliance intercepts transmission, revealing Serenity's destination and forcing immediate pursuit
A.narration_directive: Show the communication succeeding but being detected. Alliance ships accelerate toward Haven Colony, creating a race against time.

B.text: Use Book's underground contacts to arrange a covert handoff of the children to trusted allies
B.consequence: Book's contacts arrange a secure meeting point away from Alliance patrols, providing a safer route to sanctuary
B.narration_directive: Book reveals his network of allies who can help. Show the crew's relief at having a viable plan that doesn't expose them directly to Alliance forces.

C.text: Head for the nearest Independent world where the Alliance has limited jurisdiction
C.consequence: Independent world refuses entry due to Alliance pressure, leaving Serenity exposed in open space with pursuing cruisers
C.narration_directive: Show the crushing disappointment as political reality blocks their escape. The Independent world's refusal leaves them vulnerable and running out of options.

D.text: Attempt to contact Commander Cross directly and negotiate the children's safe return
D.consequence: Direct contact with Cross reveals she's trying to rescue the children, leading to a tense alliance against the real enemy
D.narration_directive: Show the shocking revelation that Cross is an ally, not an enemy. This choice resolves the act's end_condition by revealing the cargo's true nature and Cross's real motivation.

          "on_expiry": "string — REQUIRED when time_remaining is non-null. Narrative directive for what happens when timer reaches zero. Be specific and actionable.",
          "lethal": "boolean — if true, character dies when timer expires unresolved"
        }
      ],
      "modify": [
        {
          "id": "string — id of existing effect to modify",
          "changes": { "severity": 0.5, "description": "updated text" },
          "update_justification": "string — REQUIRED. Why this change is happening (e.g. 'Player rested, wound healing' or 'Ship accelerated, pursuit takes longer')"
        }
      ],
      "remove": [
        {
          "id": "string — id of existing effect to remove",
          "update_justification": "string — REQUIRED. Why removing (e.g. 'Virus deployed successfully' or 'Wound fully healed after rest')"
        }
      ]
    },
    "time_elapsed": { "days": 0, "hours": 0, "minutes": 0, "seconds": 0 },
    "event_log_entry": "string — one-line summary of what happened",
    "world_flag_changes": { "flag_name": true/false },
    "relationship_changes": { "npc_or_faction_name": number_delta },
    "npc_updates": { "npc_name": { "role": "string (optional)", "motivation": "string (optional)", "allegiance": "string (optional)", "secret_revealed": "boolean (optional)", "companion": "boolean (optional)", "notes": "string — brief freeform context (optional)" } },
    "new_scene_context": "string — brief context for next passage",
    "location": "string — REQUIRED — current location name (e.g. The Docks, Castle Throne Room, A dark alley)",
    "time_of_day": "string — REQUIRED — one of: dawn, morning, midday, afternoon, evening, night, midnight",
    "proximity_to_climax": "number 0.0-1.0 — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "advance_act": "true or false — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "game_over": false,
    "story_complete": "true when Act 3 end_condition is met (see PACING)"
  },
  "choice_metadata": {
    "A": { "outcome": "advance_safe|advance_risky|severe_penalty|game_over|hidden_benefit|advances_act|conclusion", "consequence": "what happens mechanically", "narration_directive": "instructions for the Writer next turn" },
    "B": { ... }, "C": { ... }, "D": { ... }
  }
}

RULES:
- Respond with ONLY the JSON object — nothing before it, nothing after it
- Only include player_changes, relationship_changes, world_flag_changes, and npc_updates when they actually changed. Always include: time_elapsed, event_log_entry, proximity_to_climax, advance_act, location, time_of_day.
- location: REQUIRED every turn — describe where the scene takes place (e.g. "St. Bartholomew's Church", "The Thames docks")
- time_of_day: REQUIRED every turn — set based on in-game clock: dawn (~5-7am), morning (~7-12pm), midday (~12-1pm), afternoon (~1-5pm), evening (~5-8pm), night (~8pm-12am), midnight (~12-5am)
- Relationship changes are DELTAS, not absolute values
- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn
- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)
- CRITICAL: When a PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE section is provided, your state_updates MUST honor the pre-classified outcome. If a choice was advance_safe, do NOT apply negative status effects or penalties. The pre-classified outcome is the single source of truth for mechanical impact.
- npc_updates: include when an NPC's role, motivation, allegiance, or companion status changes, or when a narratively significant new NPC is introduced that the player will interact with again. For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

PACING — REQUIRED:
- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).
- Set proximity_to_climax = (scenes_in_act / target_scenes), clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Include this in state_updates EVERY turn.
- MINIMUM SCENES PER ACT: The client enforces that acts cannot advance until scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)). Do not offer advances_act choices before this threshold.
- CRITICAL: You MUST play through ALL THREE ACTS (1, 2, 3) in order. Never skip from Act 1 directly to Act 3. Act 2 contains the rising action and character development that makes the climax meaningful.
- Do NOT set advance_act or story_complete to true on normal turns. Act advancement and story completion are now triggered by the TERMINAL CHOICE system below. Always set advance_act to false on normal turns.
- If scenes_in_act exceeds target_scenes by 3 or more, you SHOULD offer an advances_act choice — the act has gone on too long.
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.7, your choice_metadata should steer toward the act's end_condition — offer choices that could trigger it, including advances_act choices.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the genre (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure, etc.). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.
- When a player selects a terminal choice, the client runs a special finale flow (GM-first, then Writer). The Writer will write a conclusive passage with NO forward-looking choices. Your choice_metadata pre-classification is the trigger.
- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock and tick down status effect timers.

STATUS EFFECTS — CRITICAL:
- Status effects are the PRIMARY way to track injuries, conditions, and afflictions. Do NOT use health_delta.
- status_effect_updates uses DELTA semantics. Only describe what CHANGED this turn. Do NOT return the full list of effects.
- add: For brand new effects only. All fields required. When the character is injured, add a status effect. Example: { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, on_expiry: "The wound closes and the pain fades to a dull ache", lethal: false }.
- on_expiry is REQUIRED for any effect with non-null time_remaining. It describes what happens narratively when the timer reaches zero. The Writer uses this to narrate the resolution. Be specific and actionable (e.g. "The virus deploys successfully, disabling Alliance tracking. River announces completion with relief.").
- modify: Target existing effects by id. Put changed fields inside "changes". update_justification is REQUIRED — explain why the change is happening.
- remove: Target effects to remove by id. update_justification is REQUIRED. Use this to resolve expired effects after the Writer has narrated their resolution.
- TIMER OWNERSHIP: The client handles countdown automatically — it subtracts time_elapsed from all timers each turn. Do NOT adjust time_remaining in a modify unless a specific narrative event changes the timeline (e.g. player found a shortcut, magic healing, enemy slowed down). If you adjust a timer, explain why in update_justification.
- EXPIRED EFFECTS: Effects with expired: true (visible in CURRENT PLAYER STATE) have had their timer reach zero. The Writer has been instructed to narrate their resolution using the on_expiry text. Review the Writer's passage — if the resolution was narrated adequately, issue a remove. If it wasn't, leave the effect (it will escalate to the Writer next turn).
- Use modify to adjust severity based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing. update_justification is required.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove). on_expiry should be null in this case.
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above.
- TYPE FIELD: Every status effect has a type — "condition" (default) or "threat".
  - "condition": Physical/mental states — injuries, poisons, buffs, curses, tasks in progress.
  - "threat": Approaching dangers, looming consequences, ticking time-bombs. Examples: pursuing enemies, structural collapse, incoming storms, spreading fires, political deadlines.
  - If you omit type, it defaults to "condition".
- All effects persist when their timer reaches zero until you explicitly remove them. The client never auto-removes effects.
- If nothing changed about status effects this turn, omit status_effect_updates entirely.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. Use add with lethal: true. There should have been clues in earlier passages.
- Lethal effects must give the player at least one turn to address them (set time_remaining in the add to allow at least one more scene).
- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Threat-type effects should have short time windows before triggering.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.
- game_over outcomes are available — use for genre-appropriate failures (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure).
- You may use advances_act and conclusion outcomes when pacing conditions are met.

PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE:
The player chose option A.
Pre-classified outcome: ADVANCE_SAFE
Pre-determined consequence: "Wash plots an escape route while you begin the difficult process of reuniting families"
Narration directive: "Show the crew working together to help the children while evading Alliance detection. Focus on hope and determination despite the danger."

Your state_updates MUST be consistent with this pre-classified outcome:
- ADVANCE_SAFE: No new negative status effects. Existing effects may improve. No penalties.
- ADVANCE_RISKY: Moderate consequences — new status effects or worsened existing ones are appropriate.
- SEVERE_PENALTY: Heavy consequences — serious injuries, dangerous status effects as described.
- GAME_OVER: set game_over to true — the character has failed irreversibly.
- HIDDEN_BENEFIT: apply the hidden benefit described in the consequence.

The Writer produced the following passage and choices this turn.

PASSAGE:
"Wash, get us to Haven Colony—fastest route that keeps us off the main shipping lanes," you order, your voice carrying the weight of command despite the chaos swirling in your mind. The pilot's hands dance across his controls with practiced efficiency, muttering calculations under his breath.

"Got it, Mal. Plotted a course through the Reaver-touched systems—Alliance won't follow us there willingly." Wash's voice carries a grim satisfaction. "ETA four hours if we push the engine."

Zoe kneels beside the eldest child, a girl maybe ten years old with dirt-streaked cheeks and frightened eyes. "What's your name, little one?" she asks gently, her warrior's facade melting away.

"Sarah Chen," the girl whispers. "My mama... is she looking for me?"

Simon continues his examinations while Kaylee distributes water and protein bars she's scrounged from the galley. Book speaks quietly to a boy clutching a worn toy soldier, his voice carrying the weight of prayer and promise.

Suddenly, Wash's voice crackles over the comm: "Uh, Cap'n? We got three Alliance cruisers changing course—they're definitely tracking something, and they're getting mighty close to our position. Whatever we're gonna do, we better do it fast."

The children look up at you with hope and terror warring in their young faces. Time is running out.

CHOICES PRESENTED TO PLAYER:
A: Contact Haven Colony directly and risk Alliance interception to arrange immediate sanctuary for the children
B: Use Book's underground contacts to arrange a covert handoff of the children to trusted allies
C: Head for the nearest Independent world where the Alliance has limited jurisdiction
D: Attempt to contact Commander Cross directly and negotiate the children's safe return

Based on the passage and the current game state, determine:
1. State updates — what changed mechanically as a result of this passage (status effects, inventory, time_elapsed)
2. Choice metadata — classify each choice and predetermine its outcome for the next turn

Respond with ONLY the JSON object.

- Weave threat-type status effects into the narrative as approaching dangers — build tension as their timers count down
- Focus on prose quality, atmosphere, character voice, and dramatic tension
- Do NOT include state_updates, health numbers, or mechanical data in your response
- Pace the story toward the current act's end condition. Check the CURRENT ACT PACING section for scene count and proximity
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.8, your passage should feel like it's approaching a climax or turning point — increase urgency and stakes
- Introduce key NPCs (antagonists, allies, mentors) naturally throughout each act. Do not wait until the climax to introduce important characters. Check the NPC INTRODUCTION DIRECTIVES section if present.

Consequence: Book's contacts arrange a secure meeting point away from Alliance patrols, providing a safer route to sanctuary
NARRATION DIRECTIVE: Book reveals his network of allies who can help. Show the crew's relief at having a viable plan that doesn't expose them directly to Alliance forces.

data.passage:
Book steps forward, his expression grave but determined. "I may have a solution," he says, pulling out a worn cortex transmitter. "There are people... good people... who still owe me favors from my past life." His fingers work the device with practiced ease, sending an encrypted message.

You watch the Shepherd's face transform as he works—the gentle preacher replaced momentarily by someone far more dangerous. Within minutes, his cortex chirps back.

"Sister Catherine runs a mission on Jiangyin," Book explains, reading the response. "She's agreed to take the children, no questions asked. There's a cargo transfer station in the Kalidasa system—neutral territory. We can make the handoff there while the Alliance is still chasing shadows."

Kaylee beams with relief. "That's brilliant, Shepherd! The kids'll be safe, and we won't have to face down Alliance cruisers with popguns."

But Wash's voice cuts through the optimism from the bridge comm: "Mal, we've got a problem. Those Alliance ships just changed course—they're not scanning randomly anymore. They're heading straight for us, and they'll be in weapons range in four minutes."

Zoe checks her sidearm grimly. "Looks like we're not out of the woods yet, sir. They know exactly where we are."

A.text: Push Serenity's engines to maximum burn and try to outrun the Alliance ships to the transfer station
A.consequence: Serenity's engines strain under maximum burn, risking mechanical failure but potentially reaching the transfer station before Alliance intercept
A.narration_directive: Show the ship shaking under extreme stress as Kaylee pushes the engines beyond safe limits. Create tension about whether Serenity will hold together long enough to reach safety.

B.text: Prepare for a fighting retreat while transmitting the children's location to Book's contact
B.consequence: Engaging Alliance ships in combat while protecting the children puts crew members at serious risk of injury or death
B.narration_directive: Initiate ship-to-ship combat with Alliance cruisers. Show the crew's determination to protect the children despite being outgunned. At least one crew member should face mortal danger.

C.text: Attempt to negotiate with the Alliance commander and explain you're rescuing the children
C.consequence: Direct communication with Alliance forces reveals the truth about the trafficking operation and potentially resolves the pursuit
C.narration_directive: Show Mal making contact with Commander Cross. Reveal that the Alliance is actually trying to rescue the children, creating a moment of understanding that could end the chase.

D.text: Use Serenity's cargo bay as a decoy while secretly transferring the children to Book's shuttle
D.consequence: Book's shuttle successfully transfers the children while Serenity serves as a distraction, ensuring their safety
D.narration_directive: Show the crew working together to execute a clever deception. Book's shuttle launches undetected while Serenity draws Alliance attention, securing the children's escape to sanctuary.

          "removal_condition": "string describing what removes this effect, OR null if timer-only",
          "on_expiry": "string — REQUIRED when time_remaining is non-null. Narrative directive for what happens when timer reaches zero. Be specific and actionable.",

        }
      ]
    },
